title: Ensure App Service plan SKU supports private endpoints

input_row.title: Ensure App Service plan SKU supports private endpoints
input_row.description: Ensure that your App Service plan SKU supports private endpoints. Private endpoints provide secure access over Azure Private Link, which keeps traffic on the Microsoft backbone network and eliminates exposure to the public internet. Note that not all SKUs support private endpoints.
input_row.rationale: An appropriately configured private endpoint eliminates public exposure and helps prevent data exfiltration.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. In the Properties pane, under Hosting, next to SKU and size, ensure that the plan tier is one of the following: Basic, Standard, ElasticPremium, Premium, PremiumV2, Premium0V3, PremiumV3, PremiumMV3, IsolatedV2, IsolatedMV2, WorkflowStandard, FlexConsumption, and that the plan name is one of the following: B1, B2, B3, S1, S2, S3, EP1, EP2, EP3, P1, P2, P3, P1V2, P2V2, P3V2, P0V3, P1V3, P2V3, P3V3, P1MV3, P2MV3, P3MV3, P4MV3, P5MV3, I1V2, I2V2, I3V2, I4V2, I5V2, I6V2, I1MV2, I2MV2, I3MV2, I4MV2, I5MV2, WS1, WS2, WS3, FC1. 4. Repeat steps 1-3 for each app. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list apps: az webapp list For each app, run the following command to get the App Service plan ID: az webapp show --resource-group --name -- query appServicePlanId For each App Service plan, run the following command to get the plan SKU tier and name: az appservice plan show --resource-group --name --query "{tier:sku.tier,name:sku.name}" Ensure that the plan tier is one of the following: Basic, Standard, ElasticPremium, Premium, PremiumV2, Premium0V3, PremiumV3, PremiumMV3, IsolatedV2, IsolatedMV2, WorkflowStandard, FlexConsumption, and that the plan name is one of the following: B1, B2, B3, S1, S2, S3, EP1, EP2, EP3, P1, P2, P3, P1V2, P2V2, P3V2, P0V3, P1V3, P2V3, P3V3, P1MV3, P2MV3, P3MV3, P4MV3, P5MV3, I1V2, I2V2, I3V2, I4V2, I5V2, I6V2, I1MV2, I2MV2, I3MV2, I4MV2, I5MV2, WS1, WS2, WS3, FC1. Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list apps: Get-AzWebApp Run the following command to get the app in a resource group with a given name: $app = Get-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName -Name Run the following command to get the App Service plan ID: $app.ServerFarmId Run the following command to get the App Service plan in a resource group with a given name: $plan = Get-AzAppServicePlan -ResourceGroupname -Name Run the following command to get the plan SKU tier and name: $plan.Sku | select-object Tier, Name Ensure that the plan tier is one of the following: Basic, Standard, ElasticPremium, Premium, PremiumV2, Premium0V3, PremiumV3, PremiumMV3, IsolatedV2, IsolatedMV2, WorkflowStandard, FlexConsumption, and that the plan name is one of the following: B1, B2, B3, S1, S2, S3, EP1, EP2, EP3, P1, P2, P3, P1V2, P2V2, P3V2, P0V3, P1V3, P2V3, P3V3, P1MV3, P2MV3, P3MV3, P4MV3, P5MV3, I1V2, I2V2, I3V2, I4V2, I5V2, I6V2, I1MV2, I2MV2, I3MV2, I4MV2, I5MV2, WS1, WS2, WS3, FC1. Repeat for each app. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 546fe8d2-368d-4029-a418-6af48a7f61e5 - Name: 'App Service apps should use a SKU that supports private link'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. In the Properties pane, under Hosting, next to Name, click the App Service plan name. 4. Under Current App Service plan, next to Name, click the App Service plan name. 5. Under Essentials, next to Pricing plan, click the pricing plan name. 6. Select a pricing plan where the plan tier is one of the following: Basic, Standard, ElasticPremium, Premium, PremiumV2, Premium0V3, PremiumV3, PremiumMV3, IsolatedV2, IsolatedMV2, WorkflowStandard, FlexConsumption, and the plan name is one of the following: B1, B2, B3, S1, S2, S3, EP1, EP2, EP3, P1, P2, P3, P1V2, P2V2, P3V2, P0V3, P1V3, P2V3, P3V3, P1MV3, P2MV3, P3MV3, P4MV3, P5MV3, I1V2, I2V2, I3V2, I4V2, I5V2, I6V2, I1MV2, I2MV2, I3MV2, I4MV2, I5MV2, WS1, WS2, WS3, FC1. 7. Click Select. 8. Click Downgrade or Upgrade to confirm the change. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each app and App Service plan requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each App Service plan requiring remediation, run the following command to update the SKU: az appservice plan update --resource-group --name --sku
input_row.impact: App Service plan costs vary based on the selected SKU. • App Service on Linux pricing: https://azure.microsoft.com/en- us/pricing/details/app-service/linux/ • App Service on Windows pricing: https://azure.microsoft.com/en- us/pricing/details/app-service/windows/
input_row.default_value: The App Service plan is selected during creation.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_app_service_plan_private_endpoint_sku_supported

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- The automated program would check the SKU configuration of the App Service plans to determine if they support private endpoints.
- **Azure CLI Commands:**
  1. Use the command `az webapp list` to enumerate all web apps across the subscriptions or specified resource groups.
  2. For each web app obtained, run `az webapp show --resource-group <ResourceGroupName> --name <WebAppName> --query appServicePlanId` to retrieve the associated App Service plan ID.
  3. For each App Service plan ID, execute `az appservice plan show --resource-group <ResourceGroupName> --name <AppServicePlanName> --query "{tier:sku.tier,name:sku.name}"` to get the SKU tier and name.
  
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - A pass would indicate that the SKU tier is one of the following: Basic, Standard, ElasticPremium, Premium, PremiumV2, Premium0V3, PremiumV3, PremiumMV3, IsolatedV2, IsolatedMV2, WorkflowStandard, FlexConsumption.
  - Additionally, the SKU name must be one of: B1, B2, B3, S1, S2, S3, EP1, EP2, EP3, P1, P2, P3, P1V2, P2V2, P3V2, P0V3, P1V3, P2V3, P3V3, P1MV3, P2MV3, P3MV3, P4MV3, P5MV3, I1V2, I2V2, I3V2, I4V2, I5V2, I6V2, I1MV2, I2MV2, I3MV2, I4MV2, I5MV2, WS1, WS2, WS3, FC1.

This automated approach leverages the Azure CLI to ensure a swift and accurate assessment of compliance against the specified CIS benchmark control for Azure App Service plans.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** PARTIALLY_AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as "Automated" is appropriate given that the assessment can be performed using Azure CLI commands. However, the assessment could also benefit from integration with Azure SDK for Python to enhance automation capabilities and potentially improve efficiency and scalability.
- **Azure APIs/Azure SDK/Azure CLI:** The use of Azure CLI is valid, but the assessment could be enhanced by leveraging the Azure SDK for Python, which provides programmatic access to Azure services and could be used to automate the process more robustly.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 7

- **Automation Details:** The Azure CLI commands listed are technically correct and will retrieve the necessary information to determine if the App Service plan SKU supports private endpoints.
- **Azure SDK Methods:** The assessment does not mention the Azure SDK for Python, which could be used to achieve the same results. For example, using `azure-mgmt-web` to list web apps and retrieve App Service plan details.
- **Program Name:** The program name `azure_app_service_plan_private_endpoint_sku_supported` is descriptive but could be more specific. It should include details about the compliance check, such as "sku_compliance_check".

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Azure SDK for Python:** Consider using the Azure SDK for Python (`azure-mgmt-web`) to automate the retrieval of App Service plan details. This can provide a more integrated and flexible approach to automation.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** A more descriptive name could be `azure_app_service_plan_private_endpoint_sku_compliance_check` to clearly indicate the compliance aspect of the check.
- **Missing Considerations:** The assessment does not account for potential changes in SKU support over time. Regular updates to the list of supported SKUs should be considered to ensure ongoing compliance.

**CONFIDENCE:** MEDIUM

- The assessment is technically sound but could be improved by incorporating the Azure SDK for Python and considering future changes in SKU support.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Integrate the Azure SDK for Python to enhance automation capabilities and update the program name for clarity. Regularly update the list of supported SKUs to maintain compliance with evolving Azure services.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
The task of verifying whether an App Service plan SKU supports private endpoints can be efficiently automated using Azure CLI or Azure SDK for Python. Both methods provide programmatic access to retrieve the necessary SKU details and compare them against the compliance criteria. The first AI opinion correctly outlines the use of Azure CLI commands to achieve this, and the second AI opinion suggests enhancing this with Azure SDK for Python for potentially better integration and scalability. Given the capabilities of Azure APIs, this task is well-suited for automation.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
azure_app_service_plan_private_endpoint_sku_compliance_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **Azure Resource/Service:** App Service Plans
- **API/CLI Method:** Azure CLI (`az webapp list`, `az webapp show`, `az appservice plan show`) or Azure SDK for Python (`azure-mgmt-web`)
- **Properties to Check:** SKU tier and name (e.g., `sku.tier`, `sku.name`)
- **Pass Criteria:** SKU tier is one of Basic, Standard, ElasticPremium, Premium, PremiumV2, Premium0V3, PremiumV3, PremiumMV3, IsolatedV2, IsolatedMV2, WorkflowStandard, FlexConsumption, and SKU name is one of B1, B2, B3, S1, S2, S3, EP1, EP2, EP3, P1, P2, P3, P1V2, P2V2, P3V2, P0V3, P1V3, P2V3, P3V3, P1MV3, P2MV3, P3MV3.
- **Fail Criteria:** Any SKU tier or name outside the specified compliant list.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
The disagreement was minor, focusing on the potential use of Azure SDK for Python in addition to Azure CLI. The decision to automate remains consistent, with the option to use either tool based on specific implementation needs.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Microsoft_Azure_Compute_Services_Benchmark_v2_0_0
